stories.title: EU wants Hungary’s next leader to support Ukraine. It should be so lucky.
stories.summary: BUDAPEST — Péter Magyar, the front-runner in Hungary’s upcoming election, shows little sign of being the pro-Ukraine alternative many in Brussels are hoping for. EU leaders, increasingly frustrated with Prime Minister Viktor Orbán’s obstruction of support for Kyiv, are quietly betting a Magyar victory on April 12 could reset relations — or at least ease tensions with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy. But on substance, Magyar often sounds strikingly similar to the man he’s trying to

stories.title: Rival teams of vote observers spell trouble in Hungarian election
stories.summary: BUDAPEST — The emergence of alternative electoral monitoring groups — including a team created by allies of Prime Minister Viktor Orbán — is raising the prospect of political turmoil and contested results after Hungary’s general election on April 12. Concerns are already running high that the election result will be appealed by whoever loses, given that the campaign has been marked by smear tactics and accusations of foreign interference — something that only increases the importance of vote obs

stories.title: ‘Be serious’: European leaders reproach US for Nato threats – Europe live
stories.summary: Poland’s Donald Tusk says US withdrawal would be ‘Putin’s dream plan’, while Emmanuel Macron says Trump’s comments ‘erode the very substance’ of the alliance Back to Trump’s frustration with European allies – although it doesn’t involve a Nato member this time – Austria is the latest country to risk the US president’s wrath after a defence ministry spokesperson confirmed it denied all US requests for military overflights related to the Iran war. “There have indeed been requests and they were ref

stories.title: The Guardian view on Ukraine’s perilous spring: Europe’s steadfast support is more vital than ever | Editorial
stories.summary: As the world’s attention is drawn to the Middle East, Donald Trump’s assault on Iran has had damaging knock-on consequences for Kyiv After meeting European Union foreign ministers this week, Volodymyr Zelenskyy voiced exasperation over the continued blocking of a €90bn EU loan to Kyiv by Hungary’s prime minister, Viktor Orbán. US financial support for Ukraine has dried up under Donald Trump, so the money is desperately required. But as Mr Zelenskyy bitterly observed, it is being delayed “because

stories.title: Hungary's government accused of spying on opposition as key election nears
stories.summary: Prime Minister Viktor Orban's government used the country's intelligence service to spy on the opposition Tisza Party, says investigative platform Direkt36. What does this mean for the upcoming parliamentary election?

stories.title: ‘It’s amazing’: stolen 2,500-year-old Romanian gold helmet has been found
stories.summary: Prosecutors unveil priceless artefact in press conference after it was taken from Netherlands museum in January 2025 A priceless ancient golden helmet from Romania that was stolen last year from a museum in the Netherlands has been recovered, Dutch authorities have said. Under the guard of balaclava-wearing police, prosecutors unveiled the 2,500-year-old Coțofenești helmet during a news conference on Thursday in the eastern Dutch city of Assen. Continue reading...

stories.title: “They know that there'll be a Tisza government for the next four years” – a day with Péter Magyar
stories.summary: We spent an entire day on the campaign trail with Péter Magyar. Our crew accompanied the Tisza leader to six campaign events, and rode in the car with him the entire time.

stories.title: Peter Magyar, the former Orban ally vying for power in Hungary
stories.summary: Magyar represents the biggest threat to Viktor Orban's rule in Hungary since he won the first of four consecutive victories in 2010.

stories.title: Ádám ujja végét levágta egy tálca a gödi Samsung-gyárban, a cég arra kérte, hogy otthoni balesetnek jelentse
stories.summary: Ádám a baleseti táppénzt sem kapta meg, csak betegszabadságra küldték, és legalább egy évig nem adták le a hatóságnak a baleseti jegyzőkönyvet. Hogy miért, arra többféle magyarázat is szóba jöhet, de egyik sem sejtet sok jót.

stories.title: Több mint egy év eltelt, de még mindig nem hallgattak ki gyanúsítottat az újdörögdi kézigránátos baleset ügyében
stories.summary: Az MSZP-s Gurmai Zita kérdezte a legfőbb ügyészt, sikerült-e megállapítani, hogy kié a felelősség a Magyar Honvédség gyakorlóterén történt balesetben.

stories.title: Ki mérgezte meg a magyar–magyar viszonyt? A levélszavazatok politikai szerepe

Közben mindkét tábor elsiklik a lényeg felett: ez a torz kép egy tudatosan felépített politikai kommunikációs termék, amelyben Orbán Viktor és a Fidesz döntötte el, ki mit lát a másikból. stories.summary: A Két Hold és a Pagony kiadványait több mint négyezer nevező közül választotta ki a Bolognai Gyerekkönyvvásár zsűrije.

A lap szerint magyar kreatív szakemberek által létrehozott 6000 négyzetméteres immerzív színházi előadás Budapest kötelező, kihagyhatatlan látnivalójává vált.

Fox News, the New York Post, Sinclair Broadcast Group’s the National

stories.title: ‘No plan, no legal basis’: Merz sparks backlash over desire to send Syrians home
stories.summary: BERLIN — German Chancellor Friedrich Merz is facing criticism from his coalition partners after declaring that 80 percent of Syrian refugees living in Germany should leave by the end of his term in 2029. Although it’s not the first time Merz has urged Syrians to return home to help rebuild their country following its long and brutal civil war that ended in 2024, his latest remarks have drawn a wider rebuke due to their specificity and the setting in which they were made — alongside Syrian

stories.title: Feuding French Socialists leave presidential prospects in disarray
stories.summary: PARIS — Deep fissures in the French Socialist Party that hamstrung the movement during recent municipal elections now threaten to derail its hopes of winning the presidency in 2027. The once-powerful faction is grappling with an identity crisis similar to that gripping left-of-center parties across Europe . But the warring blocs in Paris can’t even agree on the process of choosing a presidential candidate for next year, let alone who should run in a contest that, for now, the far right is tipped

stories.title: Britain’s EU point man says reset deal can be done by summer
stories.summary: LONDON — A deal to re-align key parts of the U.K. economy with the European Union is on course to be reached by the early summer, according to the minister in charge of negotiations with Europe, Nick Thomas-Symonds. Britain’s Minister for European Relations told POLITICO’s Westminster Insider podcast that, despite fraught talks over tuition fees for European students and the limits of a youth mobility scheme, he has made “very significant progress” with his EU counterpart, Maroš Šefčovič. Thomas

stories.title: Trump’s tariffs have yet to result in promised US jobs boom
stories.summary: One year ago today, President Donald Trump stood at a lectern in the Rose Garden, held up a booklet of trade barriers as thick as the Bible and told a crowd of people in suits and hard hats that with his planned tariffs, “jobs and factories will come roaring back into our country and you see it happening already.” Those jobs and factories haven’t materialized, at least on the grand scale the president and his top advisers have promised. Manufacturing payrolls actually declined slightly over the

stories.title: Merz’s popularity sinks to new lows as German government support collapses
stories.summary: The popularity of German Chancellor Friedrich Merz’s government has plunged to a record low, with just 15 percent of voters satisfied with its performance, according to results published by the ARD DeutschlandTrend survey on Thursday. The figure — down nine points since early March — shows a staggering 84 percent of Germans are dissatisfied with Merz’s coalition government, which is made up of his center-right Christian Democratic Union (CDU), its Bavarian sister party, the Chris

stories.summary: Oil crisis triggered by blockade of strait of Hormuz prompts emergency measures to protect supply and halt rising prices Shrinking fuel stocks and soaring prices are leading countries around the world to burn coal, ration fuel, shorten work weeks and tell citizens to stay at home. Fossil fuel supplies have reduced since the war against Iran led to the closure of the strait of Hormuz, a crucial shipping route for oil and seaborne gas. stories.title: ‘Weak and pathetic’: why is the EU not using its leverage to stop Israel?
stories.summary: Deep divisions on Israel mean the union has failed to act over Lebanon, Gaza, or settler violence in the West Bank The human costs of Israel’s attacks on Lebanon were plain to see when the Irish MEP Barry Andrews visited Beirut last month. He met people who had fled Israeli airstrikes and complied with evacuation orders in southern Lebanon. stories.title: Denmark’s unique political model is in crisis – I blame the boomerang effect | Rune Lykkeberg
stories.summary: Voters rejected rule from the centre – but could end up with another centrist government We could be at the end of the road for the Danish method of democracy. Our style of parliamentary politics has been celebrated and admired internationally for many years, but last week’s general election has left it in crisis. stories.title: Arctic ice loss brings dual heatwaves to Europe and eastern Asia
stories.summary: The Barents Sea might seem too far away to affect our weather – but research shows it does The Barents Sea, located off the northern coasts of Norway and Russia, might seem too far away to have an impact on our weather. But new research shows that ice loss on the sea is increasing the frequency of concurrent summer heatwaves across Europe and eastern Asia. Jilan Jiang from the Chinese Academy of Meteorological Sciences in Bejing, and colleagues, studied ice data and global weather patterns going

stories.title: One killed and buildings damaged as magnitude 7.4 earthquake strikes Indonesia
stories.summary: Quake with epicentre west-north-west of Ternate island shakes cities and prompts regional tsunami warning One person has been killed after a 7.4 magnitude earthquake struck Indonesia’s Ternate island, damaging buildings and triggering small tsunami waves. The quake, which had a depth of 35km, occurred on Thursday at 6.48am local time, according to the United States Geological Survey. Its epicentre was 127km (79 miles) west-north-west of Ternate, an island in Indonesia’s North Maluku province. Co

stories.title: Coordinated conflict: how the Ukraine and Iran wars are starting to overlap
stories.summary: Battlefield outcomes are connected by the sharing of weapons and intelligence as well as the damage to the global economy The Iran and Ukraine wars are becoming more intertwined with every passing week – to the point that some analysts argue the two conflicts are beginning to merge. Quite how each war will affect the trajectory of the other is hard to predict, but it is already clear that their interconnectedness is drawing more countries into both cauldrons, extending an arc of instability that

stories.title: Archaeologists discover wreck of Danish warship sunk by Nelson 225 years ago
stories.summary: Divers in race against time to unearth wreck of the Dannebroge before seabed becomes construction site More than 200 years after being sunk by Adm Horatio Nelson and the British fleet, a Danish warship has been discovered on the seabed of Copenhagen harbour by marine archaeologists. Working in thick sediment and almost zero visibility 15 metres (49ft) beneath the waves, divers are in a race against time to unearth the 19th-century wreck of the Dannebroge before it becomes a construction site in

stories.title: US lifts sanctions on Venezuela’s acting president Delcy Rodríguez
stories.summary: US moves towards reestablishing working relations between two countries after abducting President Nicolás Maduro The US has lifted sanctions on Venezuela’s acting president, Delcy Rodríguez , in the latest step towards normalising relations between the two countries after US forces abducted her predecessor, Nicolás Maduro, and his wife. stories.title: UK braces for Storm Dave over Easter with winds up to 90mph
stories.summary: Met Office names fourth storm of the year, with weather warnings for parts of Scotland, Northern Ireland, Wales and north of England The Met Office has named its fourth storm of the year, which will bring very strong winds in the north of the UK on Saturday evening into Easter Sunday. Storm Dave will bring wind gusts of 60 to 70mph in parts of Scotland, Northern Ireland, north Wales and parts of Northern England, with a possibility of gusts of up to 90mph in some areas. Continue reading...

stories.summary: Funeral director Robert Bush had previously admitted to dozens of counts of fraud at hearing in October A fraudulent funeral director has admitted giving families the wrong ashes, lying to them, stealing from them and also stealing from charities while he was “trusted by people at a time they needed him most”. stories.title: Festivalgoers’ urine to fertilise trees in Brecon Beacons restoration scheme
stories.summary: Waste of 700 Boomtown festival attendees used to produce 540 litres of fertiliser for native tree project Scientists are aiming to grow 4,500 trees at a national park with the help of fertiliser made from festivalgoers’ urine. The fertiliser was created by the Bristol-based startup NPK Recovery, which connected its unit to a block of toilets used by 700 revellers at Boomtown festival in Hampshire in July last year. Continue reading...

stories.title: Reform donor Nick Candy sells Chelsea mansion for reported £275m
stories.summary: Sale of Providence House believed to be the most expensive on record in London UK politics live – latest updates Nick Candy, the honorary treasurer of Reform UK and a major donor, has sold his mansion in the Chelsea district of London for a reported £275m. The property developer declined to comment on the transaction, which was first reported by Bloomberg, but it is believed to be the most expensive on record in London and one of the biggest in the world. Continue reading...

stories.title: Reform promises huge cuts to benefits but would keep pensions triple lock – UK politics live
stories.summary: Reform UK would impose ‘biggest cuts to benefits bill ever seen in history of this country’ if they came to power, says Farage Q: Do you agree with the Tories about wanting more oil and gas drilling from the North Sea? Davey says Kemi Badenoch claims she can get an extra £2.5bn in tax revenue by allowing more exploration in the North Sea. He says she is “just lying”. He says everyone knows that that is not realistic. Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump lashes out at Nato: will Europe stand up to him? - The Latest
stories.summary: Donald Trump has said he is considering pulling the US out of Nato, likening the alliance to a ‘paper tiger’. It comes after weeks of denouncements from the US president against allies for not helping to reopen the strait of Hormuz. When asked about Trump’s comments, Keir Starmer said: ‘Nato is the single most effective military alliance the world has ever seen’ and ‘whatever the noise, I’m going to act in the British national interest in all the decisions that I make’. Lucy Hough speaks to the

stories.title: ‘System malfunction’ causes robotaxis to stall in the middle of the road in China
stories.summary: Distressed riders who were stranded for hours say Apollo Go customer service agents offered ‘useless platitudes’ A “system malfunction” has caused several self-driving robotaxis to stall in the middle of the road in China, police have confirmed, after distressed riders were stranded for hours. Local authorities in the central Chinese city of Wuhan said they began receiving calls “one after another” on Tuesday night from riders reporting that autonomous vehicles operated by the Chinese internet c

stories.title: Israel hits Iran with waves of attacks and says it killed top Hezbollah commander
stories.summary: War shows little sign of easing despite Donald Trump claiming Iranian leadership ‘just asked’ for ceasefire Middle East crisis – live updates Israel unleashed two waves of attacks on Tehran and said it had killed a senior Hezbollah commander on Wednesday with little sign of the war easing up despite Donald Trump repeating a claim that Iran’s leadership was seeking a ceasefire. The US president, writing on social media, said that Iran’s president had “just asked” for a ceasefire and that American

stories.title: Top Iranian official injured in strike on Tehran – as it happened
stories.summary: This liveblog is closed, please follow our new liveblog here Houthi forces in Yemen have claimed responsibility for a missile attack on southern Israel this morning, saying it was a joint operation with Iran and Hezbollah. In a statement, the Houthi movement said it carried out its third missile attack in the conflict “in conjunction with Iran and Hezbollah in Lebanon”. Continue reading...

stories.title: Iran war: Trump says US will 'finish the job' in Iran
stories.summary: President Trump said the US' "core strategic objectives" in Iran war are "nearing completion." He also urged other countries to show "courage" and "take care" of the Strait of Hormuz. DW has more.

stories.title: Indonesia: Strong earthquake strikes Molucca ⁠Sea, killing 1
stories.summary: A major 7.4-magnitude earthquake has jolted northern Indonesia, killing at least one person. An initial tsunami warning has since been lifted.

stories.title: US lifts sanctions on Venezuela's acting President Delcy Rodriguez
stories.summary: Washington has removed sanctions on interim Venezuelan leader Delcy Rodriguez. The move comes as the US continues to gradually ease measures on Venezuela after toppling Nicolas Maduro.

stories.title: UNESCO World Heritage sites facing the heat
stories.summary: From Easter Island to the Great Wall of China, many of the world's most iconic cultural heritage sites are under threat from rising temperatures linked to climate change.

stories.title: How US 'Liberation Day' tariffs reshaped global trade
stories.summary: One year after Trump's "Liberation Day" tariffs, global trade flows have shifted. Trade data shows which countries gained, which lost — and who is footing the bill.

stories.title: Iran war: Oil prices jump, stocks fall after Trump speech
stories.summary: Donald Trump said US forces would bring Iran "back to the Stone Ages" as he addressed the American people on the war. Oil prices surged following his speech, while Iran vowed a "crushing" response. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Wealth tax: Why are countries afraid to tax the ultrarich?
stories.summary: Growing income inequality and public displays of billionaire power are increasing calls for new income and wealth taxes aimed at the world's richest individuals. Can wealth ever be taxed fairly?

stories.title: Germany news: Lufthansa marks centenary amid testing times
stories.summary: Germany's national air carrier, Lufthansa, is officially 100 years old just ahead of the centenary of its first flight. Meanwhile, annual Easter peace marches get underway against the backdrop of a new war. DW has more.

stories.title: 'Drill baby drill': Trump opens wilderness to big energy
stories.summary: America's beloved national parks and public lands face conservation rollbacks and sell-offs as the Trump administration pushes for fossil fuel and timber extraction.

stories.title: Germany: Chancellor Merz has never been more unpopular
stories.summary: The CDU/CSU and SPD made many promises to voters. But according to polls, Germans are deeply disappointed with the governing coalition and have little hope that things will improve.

stories.title: Semenya hits out at impact of Olympic ban on DSD athletes
stories.summary: Caster Semenya has had her gender scrutinized in public for years after winning two Olympic golds. She has hit out at what she sees as a policy that disproportionately affects athletes with sexual differences.

stories.title: Denmark: Warship sunk by Nelson's British fleet discovered
stories.summary: Over 200 years after being sunk by the British Royal Navy under Admiral Horatio Nelson, one of Denmark's most famous warships has been discovered at the bottom of Copenhagen Harbor — complete with some remarkable items.

stories.title: How long can Iran's asymmetric strategy hold?
stories.summary: After many years of sanctions, and weeks of US-Israeli bombing, the Iranian regime is shaken — but still very much capable of threatening the region.

stories.title: US Supreme Court debates citizenship, with rare Trump visit
stories.summary: Donald Trump is the first president to appear at a Supreme Court oral argument, as he pushes to end birthright citizenship in the US.

stories.title: Trump leaves key questions unanswered as he seeks to calm nerves over Iran war
stories.summary: There were some glaring omissions in the president's primetime address, writes the BBC's Gary O'Donoghue.

stories.title: Magnitude 7.4 earthquake hits off Indonesia, killing one
stories.summary: The quake struck between the islands of Manado and Ternate. Tsunami warnings have now been lifted.

stories.title: Australia to crack down on gambling ads after years of criticism
stories.summary: Australians lose more money to gambling, per capita, than anywhere else in the world.

stories.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX set to go public in $1 trillion share listing
stories.summary: The company’s public stock debut is set to be one of the most valuable in history, and could make Musk the world's first trillionaire.

stories.title: Man dies in storm as Saharan dust shrouds Crete
stories.summary: A man dies in flooding near Athens as rain lashes several regions in Greece, while a Saharan dust storm enveloped Crete.

stories.title: Brazil grants joint custody over pets in new law for separated couples
stories.summary: Lawmakers in the Brazilian Congress on Tuesday viewed the law change as a reflection on the importance people place on their pets.

stories.title: Over 1,800 killed since junta seized power in Burkina Faso, rights group says
stories.summary: Human Rights Watch says Capt Traoré, other military leaders and jihadists "may be liable" for the killings.

stories.title: Artemis II is in orbit - what happens next?
stories.summary: What are the mission's four astronauts doing and when will they go to the Moon?

stories.title: Iconic 19th Century painting sets Indian art record with $17.9m sale
stories.summary: Yashoda and Krishna, painted by Raja Ravi Varma, beat a record set by an MF Husain work last year.

stories.title: Macron calls Trump's remarks on his marriage 'inelegant'
stories.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron has said Donald Trump's comments about his marriage were "neither elegant nor up to standard".

stories.title: Iranian Nobel laureate suffered suspected heart attack in prison, family says
stories.summary: Narges Mohammadi's brother says he fears her life is in imminent danger and she needs to be transferred to a hospital.

stories.title: Four children stabbed to death at Ugandan school
stories.summary: The suspect is in custody but his motive is unknown, the police say in a brief statement.

stories.title: What do Trump's latest comments on leaving Nato mean for the alliance?
stories.summary: Trump has criticised Nato members for showing a lack of support for US objectives in Iran.

stories.title: US Supreme Court appears sceptical of Trump plan to limit birthright citizenship
stories.summary: Trump attended the arguments in person, a rare move by a sitting president.

stories.title: Germany: Conservatives link immigration with crime
stories.summary: Will German citizens feel safer if more immigrants are deported? Chancellor Friedrich Merz has been giving that impression. But the reality is more complex.

stories.title: Reeves criticises Trump for starting Iran war with no ‘clear plan’ to get out of it – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed Starmer says he understands why people are concerned about the cost of living. He says he has already set out a five-point plan to deal with the crisis. Just look at what’s happening today. Today your energy bills will be cut because of the action that we took at the budget. And whatever happens in Iran, that price is now fixed until July. The most effective way we can support the cost of living in Britain is to push for de-escalation in the Middle East, and a reopening o

stories.title: World Cup 2026: Iraq qualify but Iran doubts loom
stories.summary: Iraq have overcome tough circumstances to reach the World Cup for the first time in 30 years, with the lineup now complete. But Iran's potential withdrawal still hangs over a tournament fraught with political issues.

stories.title: Germany: Hope fades for stranded humpback whale's survival
stories.summary: Local authorities have announced that they will cease efforts to guide a young humpback whale to its home waters. Now a restricted area has been established to allow it to "die in peace."

stories.title: NASA's Artemis II rocket lifts off for the moon
stories.summary: The four astronauts aboard the Artemis II are headed toward a historic journey around the moon. The test flight marks a major step in NASA's ambitious program of establishing a long-term presence on the lunar surface.

stories.title: Utah teen identified as victim of serial killer Ted Bundy
stories.summary: New DNA testing has solved the 51-year-old case of murdered teenager Laura Ann Aime, according to investigators.

stories.title: Watch the moment Artemis II blasts into space on historic mission
stories.summary: After delays and technical issues, the first crewed Moon mission in 50 years finally took off from Florida and is now in Earth's orbit.

stories.title: We entered Race Across the World to honour dying wish
stories.summary: Margo Oakley and her brother-in-law Mark Blythen bonded when their sister and wife Julia was terminally ill.

stories.title: Alleged Bondi gunman loses court bid to suppress names of his family
stories.summary: Lawyers for Naveed Akram had argued his mother, brother and sister live in "constant fear".

stories.title: A saját írása buktatta le Unabombert, aki közel húsz évig robbantgatott az Egyesült Államokban
stories.summary: Végül az FBI javaslatára mégis publikálták a szöveget, abban bízva, hogy valaki felismeri az írás stílusát. Ez a számítás bejött.

stories.title: Trump az amerikaiakhoz intézett beszédben kemény csapásokat ígért Iránban a következő 2-3 hétben
stories.summary: Az amerikai elnök úgy fogalmazott, folyamatban vannak a tárgyalások is. Az új vezetőket kevésbé radikálisnak tartja, mint az előzőeket.

stories.title: Toroczkai szerint a jelöltjeik írásba adták, hogy nem lépnek vissza
stories.summary: A Mi Hazánk elnöke a törökszentmiklósi állomásán beszélt erről.

stories.title: Kína rendezné a Hormuzi-szoros ügyét, de Amerikát és Izraelt okolja a válságért
stories.summary: Mindeközben Nagy-Britannia nemzetközi csúcstalálkozót hív össze a szoros miatt, a Fülöp-szigetek pedig közvetlenül Iránnal tárgyal. Közvetítésünk a közel-keleti konfliktusról.

stories.title: Elindultak a munkagépek, mondta a Fertő tavi miniszteri biztos, de nyolc év után sem lesz strand a tónál
stories.summary: A választáson újra induló fideszes Barcza Attila márciusban egy videót posztolt, amin a háttérben valami munka folyik, de egyelőre csak az építőanyagokat helyezik át máshova.

stories.title: Publicus Intézet: Először vannak többen azok, akik szerint a Fidesz elveszíti a választást
stories.summary: A közvélemény-kutató ezúttal 9 százalékpontos különbséget mért a Tisza Párt és a Fidesz között.

stories.title: „Éppen csak felébredtünk, és hirtelen rengeni kezdett a föld” – Erős földrengés Indonéziában
stories.summary: Legalább egy ember meghalt, és épületek sora dőlt össze Indonéziában egy 7,4-es erősségű földrengés után.

stories.title: Londonban több mint kilencezren fognak szavazni április 12-én
stories.summary: A 2022-es választáshoz képest csaknem 22 ezerrel többen kérték a külképviseleti szavazást.

stories.title: Sokkal több fiatal fog szavazni a választáson – élőben Róna Dániellel
stories.summary: A Fidesz kármentésben van, a külpolitikai témák helyett Szabó Bence és Gundalf megszólalásai dominálják a közéletet. Róna Dániel szerint most már nagy meglepetés lenne, ha a Fidesz nyerne, de amíg nem fújják le a meccset, nincs eredmény. Élőben a 21 Kutatóközpont vezetőjével.

stories.title: Gulyás szerint a Gundalf-üggyel a sajtó csak egy másik ügyet akar eltussolni
stories.summary: Az Európai Bizottság kérte a védett üzemanyagár felfüggesztését, de ezt a kormány visszautasította, mondta a Miniszterelnökséget vezető miniszter. A Fidesz nem tárgyalt a Mi Hazánkkal a jelöltjeik visszaléptetéséről, hangzott el a kormányinfón.

stories.title: Három helyen visszalépteti jelöltjét az MKKP a Fidesz leváltása érdekében
stories.summary: Veszprém 3-as, Pest 14-es és Nógrád 1-es választókerületében lépnek hátra. Utóbbiban a DK jelöltje és egy független képviselő is visszalépett.

stories.title: Zuhanni kezdtek az árfolyamok, miután Trump arról beszélt, keményebb csapásokkal folytatódik az iráni háború
stories.summary: A befektetők gyorsan reagáltak, miután az amerikai elnök eloszlatta az iráni konfliktus gyors lezárásához fűzött reményeket.

stories.title: A Telexszel folytatja Krug Emília, műsorgyártó bázissal erősödünk
stories.summary: „Nem lesz trash, sem kattintásvadász, ordibálós vita” – mondja az április 8-tól saját, közéleti műsorral visszatérő műsorvezető.

stories.title: „Mindössze tíz perc áldozat minden nap, és győzni fogunk” – már telefonon is lájkolásra buzdít Rákay Philip
stories.summary: A Fidesz azt ígéri, továbbra is küldik az sms-eket és az emaileket, hogy tudják az emberek, mit kell lájkolni, osztani, kommentelni.

stories.title: A mesterséges intelligencia a jövő párkapcsolati tanácsadója? (x)
stories.summary: Nincs két ugyanolyan ismerkedés, és minden ember más-más elvárásokkal érkezik egy kapcsolatba, ezért is jöhet jól egy kívülálló, mégis nagy tudást felölelő segítség, mint a mesterséges intelligencia. Az RTL Határtalan szerelem című társkereső realityjében, a műsorvezető Köllő Babett a mesterséges intelligenciát hívta segítségül, hogy a külföldi egyedülálló szingliknek találjon tökéletes párt. Vajon ez egy új trend kezdetét jelenti?

stories.title: DNS-tesztekkel azonosították Ted Bundy egyik áldozatát, fél évszázados ügyet zártak le
stories.summary: 1974 februárja és 1978 februárja között Bundy legalább 30 nőt gyilkolt meg.

stories.title: Mesterlövész, gránátvető, 29 óra bilincsben – sajtótájékoztatót tartottak az ukrán pénzszállítók elleni rajtaütésről
stories.summary: A pénzszállítók ügyvédjének sajtótájékoztatóján levetítettek egy videót, amelyben az egyik kiutasított ukrán arról beszélt, hogyan került életveszélyes állapotba egy ismeretlen szert tartalmazó injekciótól.

stories.title: „Ne add el most a szavazatodat” – üzeni Oláh Ibolya Lázár János vécépucolós mondatára emlékeztetve
stories.summary: „Semmim sincs, csak a hangom és a szabadságom. És nincs az a pénz, amiért én ezt eladnám” – mondja videójában az énekesnő.

stories.title: Álhír volt, hogy elpusztult a világ legidősebb szárazföldi állata, Jonathan, a teknős
stories.summary: A 194 éves teknős halálát még a BBC is megírta az állatorvosára hivatkozva, de kiderült, hogy átverés az egész.

stories.title: Zsinórban a harmadik kormányinfón sem kérdezhetett a Telex
stories.summary: Pedig nagyon szerettük volna megtudni, ki az a Henry.

stories.title: Leálltak a gázszállítások Ukrajna felé, de nem a kormány miatt, hanem mert nem veszik
stories.summary: Bár lenne lehetőség szállítani, és a piaci szereplők fizettek is ezért, az ukránok egyszerűen nem vesznek gáz.

stories.title: Újabb csavar: egy Theo nevű ismeretlen üzente Gundalféknak, hogy politikai megrendelésre szállt rájuk a titkosszolgálat
stories.summary: A 444 birtokába került csetüzenetek alapján Theo előre jelezte a Tiszához köthető informatikusoknak az Alkotmányvédelmi Hivatal lépéseit. Ezek szerint tényleg rálátott Gundalfék ellen indított kémelhárítási eljárásra.

stories.title: Pártemberektől is kaptunk megkereséseket – a visszalépésekről kérdeztük a Kétfarkú Kutya Párt listavezetőjét
stories.summary: Hogyan érinti az indulásuk a kormányváltás esélyeit? Lesznek még visszalépések? A Telex választási műsorában ezekről is kérdeztük a Kétfarkú Kutya Párt listavezetőjét.

stories.title: Trump most Macronon gúnyolódott, aki szerinte „még mindig lábadozik” a feleségétől kapott ütésből
stories.summary: Az amerikai elnök a tavalyi reptéri videón élcelődött egy munkaebéden, ahol az iráni háborúról tárgyaltak.

stories.title: Tolatás közben kisiklott egy mozdony a Soroksári úti állomáson
stories.summary: Senki nem sérült meg, a MÁV szerint valószínűleg emberi mulasztás történt.

stories.title: Tovább terjeszkedne a gödi Samsung SDI, 30 hektárnyi területre napelemparkot és némi fásítást is tervez
stories.summary: A koreai cég 4,2 milliárd forintos előszerződést kötött az önkormányzattal külterületi telkekre.

stories.title: Those who expect Fidesz to lose the election are a majority for the first time, survey finds
stories.summary: Publicus' latest survey measured a 9-percentage-point gap between the Tisza Party and Fidesz.

stories.title: Putyin az olcsó orosz gázzal próbál hatni az Európai Unió felé kacsintgató Örményországra
stories.summary: Putyin arról is beszélt az örmény miniszterelnöknek a Moszkvai találkozójukon, hogy nem lehetnek egyszerre az Európai Unió és az oroszok által vezetett Eurázsiai Gazdasági Unió tagja.

stories.title: Nyírbogát polgármestere maga is kérte, hogy indítsák el ellene az etikai eljárást
stories.summary: Rizsák Ildikót A szavazat árában azzal vádolták, hogy csak olyannak ír fel gyógyszert, aki fideszes. A polgármester-háziorvost felháborítják a vádak, szerinte a pozícióhalmozásában sincs összeférhetetlenség.

stories.title: Asia ramps up use of dirty fuels to cover energy shortfall triggered by Iran war
stories.summary: South Korea will delay the shutdown of coal-fired plants, while the Philippines also plans to boost the output of its coal-burning plants Governments across Asia are ramping up their use of coal, the dirtiest fossil fuel, as they try to cover huge energy shortfalls triggered by the US-Israel war on Iran. The move has triggered warnings from climate experts who point to coal’s devastating environmental impact, and say the energy crisis should be a wake up call for governments to invest in renewab

stories.title: Serbian local elections: Is Vucic's party losing its grip?
stories.summary: Serbia's ruling SNS once again secured victories in local elections — but sometimes just by a few hundred votes. Narrowing margins suggest a shifting political landscape and growing momentum for anti-government protests.

stories.title: Russian military plane crash in Crimea kills at least 29
stories.summary: The plane crashed in a mountainous forest area not far from Crimea's capital, Simferopol. Since the Kremlin sent troops into Ukraine, incidents involving Russian military planes have occurred frequently.

stories.title: Stephen Lewis, Canadian politician and social activist, dies aged 88
stories.summary: Lewis’s son Avi Lewis was elected leader of progressive New Democratic party a day before his father’s death Stephen Lewis, the Canadian diplomat, politician and human rights advocate, who spent decades tirelessly working to focus global attention on the HIV/Aids epidemic, has died of cancer. Lewis, who served as the Canadian ambassador to the United Nations, as well as the head of Ontario’s New Democratic party (NDP), was 88. Continue reading...

stories.title: Molly the border collie rescued after a week waiting for injured owner in New Zealand’s remote backcountry
stories.summary: A rescue mission involving volunteer helicopter crew and public donations ended in joy after Molly was located and brought home A spot of furry black and white appears among the jagged rocks of New Zealand’s alpine backcountry. It is Molly the border collie, sitting near the foot of a waterfall where she had been separated from her owner one week earlier. Molly was rescued on Tuesday after an avalanche of donations from the public funded a volunteer team made up of former helicopter pilots and c

stories.title: Finnish up! Claire Aho’s colour revolution – in pictures
stories.summary: The pioneering Nordic artist brought wit, verve and cinematic flair to postwar photography. A new exhibition celebrates her vibrant visual style Continue reading...

stories.title: Mediterranean sharks are vanishing in a legal void
stories.summary: Along Libya's coast, sharks — including breeding females — are sold unchecked. With little enforcement and growing financial pressure on fishermen, vulnerable species are spiraling toward extinction.

stories.title: Tiger Woods 'stepping away' for treatment after DUI arrest
stories.summary: Tiger Woods has announced a break from golf four days after he crashed his SUV and was arrested for driving under influence. Fans had been awaiting his return at the Masters this month.

stories.title: Germany's Merz sees backlash over Syrian refugee comment
stories.summary: According to Chancellor Friedrich Merz, 80% of Syrians living in Germany are to return to their home country within the next three years. The chancellor's remark has drawn fierce criticism.

stories.title: Germany: Conservatives call cannabis legalization 'a nonstarter' as 2-year report is filed
stories.summary: Conservatives see a two-year report on Germany's partial legalization of cannabis as evidence that it's been a failure. While the findings may be more nuanced, researchers did identify several problems.

stories.title: 'It's phenomenal!': Excitement in Florida ahead of Artemis launch
stories.summary: BBC’s Leyla Khodabakhshi speaks to locals and visitors in Florida ahead of the Artemis II mission blast off.

stories.title: At least 70 people killed and 30 injured in Haiti gang attack
stories.summary: Nearly 6,000 people forced to flee, human rights group says, as it criticises ‘abandonment’ from authorities At least 70 people have been killed and 30 injured during an attack in Haiti’s breadbasket Artibonite region, significantly more than official estimates, a human rights group has said. Police initially reported 16 dead and 10 injured, while a preliminary report from civil protection authorities suggested 17 had died and 19 were wounded. Continue reading...

stories.title: Sad faces all round as Bolivia’s clowns protest over decree threatening their livelihoods
stories.summary: Clowns in Bolivia are upset by mandate that stops schools hosting events from which they earn a living Dozens of clowns have marched through the streets of Bolivia’s capital to protest against a government decree that limits extracurricular activities in schools, threatening their livelihoods. Wearing full face paint and their signature red noses, the clowns gathered on Monday in front of the ministry of education in La Paz to oppose a decree published in February. The new mandate says schools m

stories.title: Germany hosts Syria's Al-Sharaa despite human rights issues
stories.summary: Germany is keen for Syrian refugees to return home. The visit by interim Syrian President al-Sharaa highlights shifting policies on repatriation and regional stability — despite accusations of human rights violations.

stories.title: Finnish police say stray Ukrainian drone carried warhead
stories.summary: Finnish authorities are investigating two drones that crashed in southern Finland. Kyiv apologized for the incident, with Ukraine's Zelenskyy saying he and Finland's Stubb are on the same page.

stories.title: Spheres of influence: the Bauhaus’s radical female photographers – in pictures
stories.summary: The images are famous, but the women who took them are often forgotten. An inspiring exhibition focuses on the pioneering ‘new vision’ of Marianne Brandt, Lucia Moholy and more Continue reading...

stories.title: UK politics: Trump says UK’s aircraft carriers are just ‘toys’ – as it happened
stories.summary: The comments were part of a broader address in which he condemned Nato allies Yesterday the Conservative party said that it wanted to ban political parties from distributing campaign literature in a foreign language. Announcing a plan to propose an amendement to the representation of the people bill to make this law, the shadow communities minister Paul Holmes said: Campaigning in a foreign language as the Greens did in Gorton and Denton only fosters greater division. A coherent national culture

stories.title: The pleasure of books in the digital age
stories.summary: The digitization of global literature has brought vast libraries to our fingertips. But the quiet power of a physical book offers a rare escape from constant digital noise.

stories.title: Germany: Stranded humpback whale moving again
stories.summary: Having barely budged the previous night, the whale stranded off Germany's Baltic coast for a week finally moved again late Monday. But after showing signs of deterioration, experts aren't sure where it's going next.

stories.title: How to fix Germany's costly health care system
stories.summary: A special commission has presented a raft of proposals aimed at curbing Germany's spiraling health care costs. But whether the government can impose them is another matter.

stories.title: Air Canada CEO to retire after English-only condolence furor
stories.summary: Air Canada has announced that CEO Michael Rousseau will retire later this year. This follows public criticism of his failure to voice condolences in French as well as English after two pilots were killed in a collision.

stories.title: Germany beat Ghana to complete confidence-boosting March
stories.summary: A late winner from Stuttgart's Deniz Undav secured a second friendly win in a week for Germany. With the FIFA World Cup just 10 weeks away, Julian Nagelsmann's team will be starting to believe they can win it all.

stories.title: After the Iran war, how fast could global trade recover?
stories.summary: The Iran war could end soon or drag on for much longer. Whenever the Strait of Hormuz is reopened, the true test will be how quickly energy, fertilizer and other supply chains can bounce back.

stories.title: Were Grandma and Grandpa Nazis?
stories.summary: What did our forebears do during the Nazi era? A subject long shrouded in silence in German families. Now, the US National Archives has made millions of NSDAP membership cards available online.

stories.title: Inside Nasa's moon spacesuit lab ahead of Artemis launch
stories.summary: The Artemis II spacesuits are designed to protect the astronauts during launch and landing, as well as possible emergency scenarios.

stories.title: KP Sharma Oli: Nepal’s former prime minister arrested over alleged role in deadly protest crackdown
stories.summary: At least 77 people killed in anti-corruption youth uprising in September, which began over a brief social media ban Nepal’s former prime minister KP Sharma Oli was arrested early on Saturday morning over his alleged role in the deaths of dozens of people who took part in the gen Z protest that toppled his government last year. Police detained the three-time former prime minister at his residence in the capital Kathmandu, and also arrested his former home affairs minister Ramesh Lekhak. Continue

stories.title: Russian students duped into signing contracts with military
stories.summary: University students are being recruited to serve in drone units fighting Ukraine. They are offered lucrative contracts and promised they won't be sent to the front. Reports suggest those promises are not always kept.

stories.title: Finland reports drone crashes, alleges territorial violation
stories.summary: Two unidentified drones crashed in southern Finland. According to the country's prime minister, the drones may have been Ukrainian drones that strayed into Finnish airspace.

stories.title: Belgium's state-owned railway shines light on Holocaust role
stories.summary: A Holocaust survivor and the son of a Nazi collaborator have shared their experiences at Brussels' Train World museum. Talking to students, they warned of the dangers of forgetting history.

stories.title: France: Two new arrests over foiled Bank of America attack
stories.summary: French police arrested two more suspects over a foiled attack on Bank of America's Paris offices. The custody of the first arrested suspect arrested has been extended.

stories.title: Russia wants to legalize cars stolen in EU
stories.summary: Vehicles stolen in the EU may soon be legally registered in Russia. One German police union fears this could lead to an increase in car thefts in Europe.

stories.title: Nepal’s PM-to-be uses rap to call for unity in first post-election message
stories.summary: Balendra Shah, 35, is a symbol of change in country whose government was toppled last year in youth-led uprising Nepal’s rapper turned politician Balendra Shah, who is about to be sworn in as prime minister, has issued his first post-election message in the form of a rap urging unity. Hours before the release he swore an oath as a newly elected lawmaker, and he is due to become the Himalayan republic’s new prime minister on Friday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Urgent action needed to prevent surge in digital violence in Africa, experts say
stories.summary: A huge rise in internet users under the age of 30 has fuelled an increase in online violence against women and girls with devastating real-life effects, activists say Activists and lawyers in Africa are calling for urgent action to protect women, girls and boys as digital violence surges across the continent. A massive rise in internet users, coupled with huge numbers of people aged under 30 , has fuelled an increase in gendered online violence across the continent, according to experts, by givi

stories.title: Weather tracker: Thunderstorms drench UAE and Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: Abnormally strong jet stream triggers deluge in Middle East, while north Africa braces for 60-80mph gusts An unusual weather pattern unleashed severe thunderstorms across parts of the Middle East last week, battering countries including the United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia. The Arabian peninsula – typically dominated by arid desert climates – received up to 150mm of rain in just a few days. The deluge was caused by an abnormally strong jet stream, which helped a deep area of low pressure to

stories.title: Nestle says thieves stole 12 tons of KitKat chocolate bars
stories.summary: "We've always encouraged people to have a break with KitKat," the company said, "but it seems thieves have taken the message too literally."

stories.title: Zelenskyy visits Doha as Ukraine, Qatar sign defense pact
stories.summary: Ukraine and Qatar have agreed to cooperate on high-tech projects and share battlefield expertise on countering missile and drone threats.

stories.title: German public sector keen to end reliance on US tech
stories.summary: Government agencies depend on US-based servers and software, making them potentially vulnerable to political pressure and even blackmail. How can Germany achieve digital sovereignty?

stories.title: Germany news: Prognosis 'not good' for humpback whale
stories.summary: Hopes that a whale stranded in shallow waters off the Baltic coast can be guided back to the ocean are fading. Meanwhile, hundreds escaped from a nightclub that became engulfed in flames.

stories.title: UK police question suspect after Derby car incident
stories.summary: Seven people were seriously injured when a car plowed into pedestrians in the central English city of Derby. A man, who is originally from India, was arrested on suspicion of attempted murder.

stories.title: Pope Leo condemns using God to justify war in Palm Sunday mass
stories.summary: The pope has said God rejects the prayers of those who wage war and have "hands full of blood." Leo has also prayed especially for Christians in the Middle East during a Palm Sunday Mass.

stories.title: Amnesty flags FIFA World Cup human rights risks
stories.summary: The human rights group has sounded the alarm about risks faced by visitors to the 2026 FIFA World Cup. It goes as far as to describe the situation in the United States as a "human rights emergency."

stories.title: ‘This is the saddest moment’: families search for loved ones on Eid after Kabul hospital strike
stories.summary: At least 400 killed in Pakistan’s strike on drug rehab centre, Taliban say, with families searching unmarked mass graves Sohrab Faqiri spent Eid, the Muslim festival to mark the end of the fasting month of Ramadan, looking for the grave of his brother, killed in a massive Pakistan airstrike on Kabul this week . Pakistan’s bombardment campaign, on what it says is terrorist and military infrastructure in neighbouring Afghanistan, appeared to have gone catastrophically wrong. A rehabilitation centr

stories.title: ‘We need to think much bigger’: trade minister calls for greater ambition in UK-EU reset
stories.summary: Exclusive: Chris Bryant says policy agreements are being done in bits and pieces but a greater vision is needed by both sides It was all smiles and warm handshakes when the two men in charge of renegotiating the UK’s relationship with the EU met in Brussels this week. Maroš Šefčovič and the UK minister for EU relations, Nick Thomas-Symonds, sharing a stage on the third floor of the vast European parliament building, were at pains to show the cross-Channel relationship was in a good place after y

stories.title: ‘We consider every mile we drive’: how fuel shortages are affecting readers worldwide
stories.summary: From a shop owner in India to a community worker in New South Wales, rising fuel prices are forcing people to ration oil usage Middle East crisis – live updates Alagesan, 35, needs liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) to run his roadside drink and snack shop in Coimbatore, India, but with the fuel shortage since the US-Israel attacks on Iran, he worries his business could fold. “I am far away from the Middle East, but my life is affected,” he said. “The gas cylinder is not available because of the war.

stories.title: Taliban release US academic held in detention for more than a year
stories.summary: Marco Rubio welcomes release of Dennis Coyle, who was detained in January last year for violating unspecified laws Afghanistan’s Taliban authorities have released the American academic Dennis Coyle after holding him for over a year, with the foreign ministry saying the release came on the occasion of Eid al-Fitr, the Muslim holiday that marks the end of the Islamic holy month of Ramadan. A statement from the ministry said the academic researcher had been released in Kabul on Tuesday, following a
